narration: Ever wonder why we only see one side of the Moon?
visual_description: Hook visual: Ever wonder why we only see one side of the Moon?
ai_image_prompt: Hook visual: Ever wonder why we only see one side of the Moon?
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Hook visual: Ever wonder why we only see one side of the Moon?

narration: It's not hiding. The Moon is tidally locked, spinning in perfect sync with its orbit around Earth.
visual_description: Context visual: It's not hiding. The Moon is tidally locked, spinning in perfect sync with its orbit around Earth.
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: It's not hiding. The Moon is tidally locked, spinning in perfect sync with its orbit around Earth.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: It's not hiding. The Moon is tidally locked, spinning in perfect sync with its orbit around Earth.

narration: This means it takes the same time to rotate once as it does to orbit Earth — about 27.3 days.
visual_description: Evidence visual: This means it takes the same time to rotate once as it does to orbit Earth — about 27.3 days.
ai_image_prompt: Evidence visual: This means it takes the same time to rotate once as it does to orbit Earth — about 27.3 days.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Evidence visual: This means it takes the same time to rotate once as it does to orbit Earth — about 27.3 days.

narration: So, while it may seem like the Moon's playing coy, it's just how physics works!
visual_description: Escalation visual: So, while it may seem like the Moon's playing coy, it's just how physics works!
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: So, while it may seem like the Moon's playing coy, it's just how physics works!
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: So, while it may seem like the Moon's playing coy, it's just how physics works!

narration: This tidal locking creates phases, making our Moon's appearance change over time.
